text: The patient is positioned for a pelvic procedure with the robot set on the left side. Ports are placed transversely above the umbilicus. A large pelvic mass is visible upon abdominal entry and insufflation. The uterus is retracted anteriorly for better visualization. The retroperitoneum is opened to the right of the mass, and Firefly technology is used to identify the ureter. Dissection proceeds laterally to expose the right side of the mass, with the sigmoid colon displaced anterolaterally. The left ureter is visualized along the pelvic sidewall. The mass is freed circumferentially at its superior aspect, allowing for increased mobility during distal dissection.

The surgeon performs lateral and interior dissection down to the pelvic floor and distal extent of the large mass. A digital rectal exam confirms the location. Posterior dissection is challenging due to the mass's size and adhesions. After reaching the dissection limit robotically, a fan and steel incision is made to complete mobilization and remove the tumor intact.
